items.question: Which read_csv argument limits columns loaded from disk?
items.explanation: usecols accepts a list of column names or indices so pandas skips other fields during parsing.

items.question: A SKU column shows as float64 with values like 1001.0 after read_csv. What likely went wrong?
options: Column was inferred as numeric instead of string ID
items.answer: Column was inferred as numeric instead of string ID
items.explanation: CSV inference treats numeric-looking tokens as floats; reload with dtype={"sku": "string"}.

items.question: For a 10 GB Parquet file with 400 columns but 15 model features, best first step?
items.explanation: Parquet column pruning avoids reading unused fields from disk.

items.question: After loading the full dataset, which action risks leakage?
options: Computing mean imputation on all rows before splitting
items.answer: Computing mean imputation on all rows before splitting
items.explanation: Test-row values influence imputation applied to train rows when stats are global.

items.question: Which call gives the most accurate total RAM for object columns?
items.explanation: deep=True includes string contents inside object columns, not just pointer overhead.
